alignments.candidate_evidence: 8 years at Google Devices and Services with global commercial operations and transformation program leadership, but primarily in strategy and operations, not technical customer-facing roles like partner engineering or solutions architecture
alignments.job_requirement: 8+ years in technical customer-facing roles (partner engineering, solutions architecture, sales engineering)

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate profile explicitly notes 'No production software-engineering background' and 'Python basic and actively learning.' Experience is in requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout, and transformation rather than hands-on integration and debugging
alignments.job_requirement: Deep hands-on technical expertise – comfortable building with APIs, debugging integrations, guiding developers through complex implementations

alignments.candidate_evidence: No full-stack development background documented. Candidate is actively learning Python at a basic level
alignments.job_requirement: Full-stack development experience and proficiency in at least one programming language

alignments.candidate_evidence: 8 years of global commercial operations and transformation leadership implies stakeholder management and cross-functional communication, consistent with strategy and operations background
alignments.job_requirement: Strong communication and ability to bridge technical and non-technical audiences

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google Devices and Services experience leading global commercial operations and transformation programs suggests project leadership and multicultural team experience, but scope is operational rather than technical-project focused
alignments.job_requirement: Proven success leading technical projects or initiatives end-to-end in globally distributed, multicultural teams

alignments.candidate_evidence: No documented experience with payments, fintech, or commerce platforms in candidate profile
alignments.job_requirement: Strong understanding of payments, fintech, or commerce platforms

gaps.gap: No hands-on technical customer-facing background (partner engineering, solutions architecture, sales engineering) – candidate background is strategy and operations, not solutions or partner engineering
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require significant ramp in technical customer-facing disciplines and sales engineering principles; unlikely to bridge core job intent within reasonable onboarding

gaps.gap: No production software development or hands-on API/integration debugging experience – candidate is at basic Python level and actively learning
gaps.mitigation: Role requires deep hands-on technical capability; candidate's strength is transformation and requirements logic, not coding and integration troubleshooting

gaps.gap: No payments, fintech, or commerce platform domain knowledge documented
gaps.mitigation: Domain knowledge is learnable, but combined with lack of technical implementation depth, puts candidate far from role readiness on both axes

gaps.gap: Limited consulting or forward-deployed technical advisory experience – candidate's transformation work was internal to Google, not customer-facing technical advisory at merchant/partner level
gaps.mitigation: Would require reorienting from internal operations transformation to external technical advisory and customer problem-solving under time pressure

hard_blockers.evidence: JD requires '8+ years of experience in technical customer-facing roles (partner engineering, solutions architecture, sales engineering), working with enterprise merchants or global partners' and 'Deep hands-on technical expertise – comfortable building with APIs, debugging integrations, and guiding developers through complex implementations.' Candidate profile explicitly states 'No production software-engineering background' and 'Python basic and actively learning,' with experience rooted in strategy/operations transformation rather than technical customer-facing partner engineering or solutions architecture.

output.level_rationale: Role requires 8+ years in technical customer-facing disciplines (partner engineering, solutions architecture, sales engineering) with hands-on API and integration capability, suggesting a senior technical IC track (L5). The "Staff" title and "senior individual contributor" description reinforce senior-IC level. Reporting and scope context not provided in JD, but 8+ years technical depth and hands-on requirement signal L5 equivalent. Not L6 because manager-of-managers scope is absent; not L4 because technical depth and enterprise customer scope exceed mid-level IC expectations.
output.summary: This is a Staff Solutions Engineer role requiring 8+ years of hands-on technical customer-facing experience (partner engineering, solutions architecture, sales engineering) in payments or commerce, with deep API and integration debugging capability. The candidate's background is strategy and operations transformation at Google—not technical customer-facing or partner-engineering work—and explicitly lacks production software development and hands-on technical depth (basic Python, actively learning). The role demands hands-on technical advisory and integration leadership; the candidate's expertise is transformation logic, requirements design, and operational program execution. No payment/fintech/commerce domain knowledge is documented. This is a disqualifying mismatch on core technical customer-facing experience and hands-on capability.
uncertainties: Candidate's specific technical skills beyond Python and code-debugging capability are not detailed; degree/educational background in CS or engineering not stated
Extent of candidate's interaction with developer or technical partner audiences in Google transformation work is unclear
Whether candidate has any exposure to API-first platforms or integration design in prior Google roles is not documented
Block's current stage/team size not stated in JD; only Afterpay context (founded 2014, 11M customers, 64K retailers) provided
